items.text: Do you have rheumatoid arthritis, and does this pain feel like your usual flares?
choices: Known rheumatoid arthritis, feels like a typical flare
Known rheumatoid arthritis, feels different from prior flares

items.text: Are multiple joints affected on both sides of your body, or is it just one joint?
Do you have a fever or chills along with the joint pain?
Did this joint pain come on suddenly and get severe within a day or two?
choices: Gradual onset over days to weeks
Rapid onset over hours to a day

items.text: Is the affected joint too painful to move or bear weight on at all?
choices: Painful but still able to move or bear some weight
Unable to move the joint or bear weight at all

items.text: Have you had a skin infection, cut, or wound near this joint recently?
choices: No nearby skin infection or wound
Recent nearby skin infection or wound

items.text: Have you had a joint injection, joint surgery, or a joint replacement in this joint?
Are you on a biologic medication, steroids, or another medication that suppresses your immune system?
How long does your morning stiffness in this joint usually last?
Have you missed doses of your rheumatoid arthritis medication recently?
Do you inject drugs, or have you had a recent bloodstream infection?
Have you had fatigue, dry eyes, dry mouth, or rashes along with the joint symptoms?
choices: Fatigue with dry eyes or mouth

items.text: Inspect the affected joint for erythema, warmth, and swelling.
Palpate for a joint effusion.
Assess active and passive range of motion of the affected joint.
choices: Limited range of motion from pain
Refuses any passive movement due to pain

items.text: Examine contralateral and other joints for symmetric synovitis.
Assess temperature for fever.
Assess hemodynamic stability.
Inspect for a nearby skin portal of entry such as a wound, ulcer, or cellulitis.
Inspect for chronic rheumatoid deformities such as ulnar deviation or swan-neck deformity.
Auscultate the heart for a new murmur suggesting endocarditis as a source of septic arthritis.
Assess distal pulses, capillary refill, sensation, and strength distal to the affected joint.
choices: Normal distal perfusion, sensation, and strength
Diminished distal perfusion, sensation, or strength
